articles.title: Code Without Structure? TrackTime v. Amazon and the § 112(f) Redo After Dyfan
articles.content:
Dennis Crouch

本文的精彩之处在于探讨不确定性问题及其与隐含手段加功能权利要求限制之间的相互作用。地区法院认定TrackTime的美国专利第8,862,978号的权利要求9成立，但联邦巡回法院撤销原判并发回重审。核心问题是，在Williamson案之后，权利要求限制“配置为[执行各种转录使用功能的]可执行程序代码”是否应被视为手段加功能限制。

本案的核心是Dyfan, LLC诉Target Corp.案，28 F.4th 1360（联邦巡回法院2022年），该案认定所主张的“代码”与描述其操作的语言相结合时提供了结构。我在Dyfan案作出后不久曾撰写文章讨论此案。Dennis Crouch，《辨别Williamson v. Citrix的目的和手段》，Patently-O（2022年3月30日）。在TrackTime案中，地区法院认定“代码”限制缺乏结构，因此属于112(f)条款中的虚假措辞（nonce word）。但该判决在Dyfan案之前作出。Dyfan案并未确立代码始终具有结构性的规则——具体情境至关重要。在发回重审中，地区法院需要进行重新分析，可能需要新证据。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

TrackTime, LLC v. Amazon.com Services LLC, No. 2024-1102 (Fed. Cir. July 2026)

The interesting bit of this decision focuses on indefiniteness and its interplay with implicit means plus function claim limitations.  The district court found asserted claim 9 of TrackTime's U.S. Patent No. 8,862,978., but the Federal Circuit vacated and remanded for reconsideration.  The basic question is whether the claim limitation "executable program code configured to [perform various transcript usage functions]" should be considered a means-plus-function limitation post-Williamson.

The centerpiece in the case is Dyfan, LLC v. Target Corp., 28 F.4th 1360 (Fed. Cir. 2022), which found the claimed "code" provided structure "when coupled with language describing its operation."  I wrote about Dyfan shortly after it issued. Dennis Crouch, Discerning the Purpose and Means of Williamson v. Citrix, Patently-O (Mar. 30, 2022).

In TrackTime, the district court found "code" limitation to lack structure and thus be a 112(f) nonce word. But, that decision came before Dyfan.  Of course, Dyfan does not create a rule that code is always structural -- the context matters. And, on remand the district court will need to conduct a fresh analysis, potentially with new evidence.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
